structure_flags.label: Bibliography
structure_flags.one_line: roth_2026_eventstudy_note is cited as a working paper, but the verified 2026 version is published in The Japanese Economic Review; update the bibkey/venue.

novelty_flags.label: Conjecture 1
novelty_flags.one_line: tier=field below novelty_target=flagship: the focal object C(pi,Sigma,G,T,a) is promised, but Exhibit 9.1 inserts contrast rows rather than deriving them from the four estimators' normal equations.

novelty_flags.label: Conjecture 2
novelty_flags.one_line: The normed variance gap depends on Omega and C_m, but the exhibit assumes Omega=I and does not compute the covariance metric from estimator-specific score primitives.

novelty_flags.label: Conjecture 3
novelty_flags.one_line: Pi_BJS is advertised as a constructive correction map, but Exhibit 9.2 only subtracts an assumed contrast C tau and does not compute a corrected loading from primitive R_m, B, pi, and Sigma.

novelty_flags.label: Conjecture 1
novelty_flags.one_line: The flagship gap cites Sun-Abraham and Callaway-Sant'Anna by bibkey only; add section/equation anchors tying those papers to the missing IF-equivalence frontier.

novelty_flags.label: Conjecture 1 | Conjecture 2
novelty_flags.one_line: The checklist misses Chen-Sant'Anna-Xie 2025 on closed-form EIFs and Roth-Sant'Anna 2021/2023 on efficient staggered rollout estimators, both closer to the IF/efficiency frontier than Roth 2026 or Gardner 2022.

soundness_flags.label: Theorem 1
soundness_flags.one_line: The theorem assumes only common target primitives, then concludes actual four-estimator IF projections differ from an exhibited C whose rows are not shown to be induced by BJS, SA, CS, or Wooldridge.

soundness_flags.label: Theorem 1
soundness_flags.one_line: Direct multiplication proves C tau != 0 for the displayed matrix, but not that the displayed matrix is the estimator contrast matrix defined in Section 6.

soundness_flags.label: Conjecture 3
soundness_flags.one_line: The correction map reads as a Moore-Penrose projection/subtraction identity after C_m is defined as the loading difference; Section 10 gives no three-step non-routine route beyond projection algebra.

strengthening_paths.suggestion: Replace Exhibit 9.1 with a derivation table: for each of BJS, SA, CS, and Wooldridge, write the finite normal equations, compute R_m, then form C from the displayed R_m-R_BJS rows.

Add location-level anchors for the closest comparator claims: BJS efficiency/imputation theorem, SA interaction-weight contamination, CS group-time ATT/DR influence function, Wooldridge imputation-pooling equivalence, and Chen-Sant'Anna-Xie EIF formulas.
Downgrade Theorem 1 to a conjectural witness unless the estimator-induced C is computed; otherwise restate it as a purely algebraic exhibit with no conclusion about published estimator IFs.
For Conjecture 3, compute Pi_BJS on the same 3-by-4 witness from primitive loadings, and verify the correction preserves a' tau while annihilating each estimator-specific contrast row.

recommended_next_step: Revise locally by deriving the contrast matrix and Omega from the four estimator equations; without that exhibit, the proposal remains field-tier and cannot satisfy the flagship floor.
